results.title: Remote ML Engineer, Ads Personalization & Discovery Job in New York, NY at Paramount Pictures
highlights:
# Remote ML Engineer, Ads Personalization & Discovery Job in New York, NY at Paramount Pictures

Published: 2026-06-07T16:11:55+00:00
Source: mediabistro.com (mediabistro.com)
Language: en

## Story

Remote ML Engineer, Ads Personalization & Discovery Job in New York, NY at Paramount Pictures

# Remote ML Engineer, Ads Personalization & Discovery

Paramount Pictures · New York, NY, USA · 2 days ago

Pay: 125.000

Job type: Full Time

Save JobApply on Company Site

---

Paramount Pictures is looking for a Machine Learning Engineer focused on Ads Personalization to join the Pluto TV team. This remote position involves developing ML components for channel ranking, optimizing user experience, and utilizing technologies like Python, SQL, TensorFlow, and Google Cloud Platform. The ideal candidate has over 3 years of experience in machine learning and is comfortable working in a dynamic environment aimed at enhancing viewer engagement. Competitive salary and benefits offered.#J-18808-Ljbffr

Apply on Company Site

results.title: Sr Director, Solution Architect & AI [Remote] | Wfh
highlights: Paramount means joining a team of passionate people who not only recognize the power of content but also enjoy a touch of fun and uniqueness. Together, we co-create moments that matter – both for our audiences and our employees – and aim to leave a positive mark on culture. Senior Director, Global Operations Source to Pay Solution Architecture & Intelligent Automation Location: Nashville, TN Reports To: SVP, Procurement Operations Department: Global Operations Center of Excellence (COE) Overview The Global Operations Center of Excellence (COE) at Paramount drives operational excellence across the enterprise. In partnership with Procurement, Accounts Payable (AP), Facilities, Security, and Order to Cash, the COE ensures consistency, compliance, and performance through scalable processes and governance. The Senior Director of Source to Pay Solutions Architecture & Intelligent Automation leads the design, integration, and governance of endtoend SourcetoPay (S2P) capabilities that drive process excellence, digital enablement, and enterprise agility. This role serves as the chief architect for S2P transformation, partnering with business leaders, technology teams, and external providers to define and implement futurestate capabilities across platforms including SAP Ariba, Oracle Fusion, and other ERP ecosystems. The role also oversees AI development initiatives within Global Operations Centers of Excellence, advancing automation, decision intelligence, and measurable efficiency gains across Procurement, Accounts Payable, Facilities, Security, and OrdertoCash. Key Responsibilities • Lead the enterprise-wide S2P architecture strategy, ensuring alignment with business objectives, operational performance goals, and technology standards. • Architect integrated workflows across sourcing, contracting, procurement, invoicing, and payment processes. • Translate complex business requirements into scalable, modular, and interoperable system designs. • Oversee integration strategies across ERP platforms, procurement tools, supplier networks, and financial systems. • Partner with IT and external vendors to ensure seamless data flow, system interoperability, and system performance optimization. • Drive automation, digitization, and workflow simplification initiatives to reduce manual effort and improve cycle times. • Establish and enforce architectural governance frameworks to ensure consistency, compliance, and scalability. • Collaborate with Technology, Legal, Compliance, and Audit teams to embed controls, regulatory requirements, and riskmitigation measures into solution design. • Serve as a strategic advisor to senior leadership on technology investments, capability roadmaps, and transformation priorities. • Lead cross-functional workshops to align business needs, technical capabilities, and change impacts. • Communicate architectural decisions and trade-offs clearly to technical and nontechnical stakeholders. • Monitor emerging technologies, industry trends, and best practices to inform future-state architecture. • Conduct system assessments, gap analyses, and maturity evaluations to identify optimization opportunities. • Oversee postimplementation reviews and drive continuous improvement across S2P processes and platforms. AI Development Responsibilities • Identify, evaluate, and prioritize AI and automation use cases across Global Operations functions. • Design and prototype AIenabled workflows leveraging LLMs, machine learning models, and automation tools integrated with ERP platforms. • Partner with data teams to ensure data readiness, quality, governance, and compliance for AI model development and deployment. • Implement responsible AI practices, including security, governance, and ethical standards aligned with corporate policies. • Define performance metrics, monitoring processes, and lifecycle management for AI solutions, including drift detection and retraining protocols. • Collaborate

results.title: Principal Engineer, Production Systems [Remote] | Hirevector
highlights: ve got the brands, we've got the stars, we've got the power to achieve our mission to entertain the planet – now all we're missing is… YOU! Becoming a part of Paramount means joining a team of passionate people who not only recognize the power of content but also enjoy a touch of fun and uniqueness. Together, we co-create moments that matter – both for our audiences and our employees – and aim to leave a positive mark on culture. In This Role You'll: We at Paramount Skydance are looking for a Principal Engineer, Production Systems! Within this team, you will join a group that designs, installs, and debugs various Broadcast Production systems. These include Audio, Video, Control, and IT related to broadcast equipment. As the Principal Engineer, you will be a critical resource, crafting and supporting everything needed to put together a show at our historic broadcast center! Responsibilities Include: • Design and support in-house video, audio and control systems. • Support maintenance department for problem-solving and repair of above-mentioned systems. • Maintain network and system documentation. • Conform to CBS security policies, initiatives, and compliance reporting. • Supervision of project as relates to wiring technicians is required. Basic Qualifications You Bring: • In depth knowledge of broadcast systems: compressed and uncompressed audio formats and video formats, with understanding of SMPTE 2110 IP design and implementation; production control systems commonly used in complex production control rooms for live and non-live applications. • Basic understanding of networking, subnetting, and VLAN implementation. • Strong knowledge of AutoCAD and Vizio. • Deep technical knowledge of current broadcast systems software, protocols, and standards. • Proven understanding of network administration. • Hands-on software and hardware troubleshooting experience. • Experience documenting and maintaining configuration and process information. Bonus Skills: • College diploma or University degree in the field of Electrical or Broadcast engineering or a minimum of 7 years of equivalent work experience. • Excellent listening, interpersonal, written, and oral communication skills. • Ability to optimally prioritize and complete tasks in a fast paced environment. • Skilled at working within a team-oriented, collaborative environment. • Ability to conduct research into hardware and software issues and products as required. • Experience with Microsoft Excel and Word. Paramount Skydance Corporation (NASDAQ: PSKY) is a leading global media and entertainment company that creates premium content and experiences for audiences worldwide. Driven by iconic studios, networks and streaming services, Paramount's portfolio of consumer brands includes CBS, Showtime Networks, Paramount Pictures, Nickelodeon, MTV, Comedy Central, BET, Paramount+, and Pluto TV, among others. Paramount delivers the largest share of the US television audience and boasts one of the industry's most important and extensive libraries of TV and film titles. In addition to offering innovative streaming services and digital video products, the company provides powerful capabilities in production, distribution and advertising solutions. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION Hiring Salary Range: $170,000.00 - 195,000.00. The hiring salary range for this position applies to New York City, California, Colorado, Washington state, and most other geographies. Starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to geographic location, market demands, experience, training, and education. The benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage, disability benefits, tuition assistance program and PTO or, if applicable, as otherwise dictated by the appropriate Collective Bargaining Agreement. This position is bonus eligible. What We Offer: • Attractive

results.title: Paramount Hires Head of Consumer AI to Lead Tech
highlights:
Paramount Hires Head of Consumer AI to Lead Tech
[...]
Paramount is pushing further into artificial intelligence, with the David Ellison-led entertainment company hiring a new head of consumer AI on Monday.
[...]
Dane Glasgow, the former Meta executive who joined Paramount as chief product officer last year, announced the hiring of Barak Turovsky in a memo to staff, which was obtained by The Hollywood Reporter. Turovsky will be executive VP and head of consumer AI for the company
[...]
“He joins at a critical time in our company’s evolution as we look to scale AI and ML to meaningfully drive our growth,” Glasgow wrote in his memo. “He will be responsible for growing our AI / ML team and their capacity as we attract some of the best talent available. Barak and team will also explore ways to leverage AI to improve personalization, content discovery, platform intelligence, consumer engagement, and monetization capabilities across Paramount+ and Pluto TV.”
[...]
Turovsky was most recently chief AI officer for General Motors, and before that worked in AI roles at Cisco, Trax, and Google, where he spent more than a decade.
[...]
Now the company has a dedicated AI exec who will be tasked with bringing that tech to life within he company. You can read Glasgow’s memo, below.
[...]
FROM: Office of Dane GlasgowSL: Leadership Update – Welcome Barak!Team,As we see every day, the pace of technological change our industry is experiencing is rapid and we’ve already made tremendous progress in recent months to evolve our organization and products to remain on the forefront of what’s possible. In order to supercharge our transformation, I am excited to announce that Barak Turovsky is joining Paramount as our EVP and Head of Consumer AI, reporting directly to me. Barak brings deep and highly relevant experience, having contributed to the development of foundational technologies that underpin AI and applying those capabilities at scale across multiple industries. At Google, he led the Languages AI product organization responsible for commercializing AI technologies across Google Search, Ads, YouTube, Cloud, Translate, Android, and other products, and spearheaded the product efforts behind the industry’s first large-scale commercial deployment of large language models and Transformers-based technology. Later, he held senior AI-focused roles at both General Motors and Cisco leading research, engineering and product teams to develop cutting-edge solutions and technologies. He joins at a critical time in our company’s evolution as we look to scale AI and ML to meaningfully drive our growth. He will be responsible for growing our AI / ML team and their capacity as we attract some of the best talent available. Barak and team will also explore ways to leverage AI to improve personalization, content discovery, platform intelligence, consumer engagement, and monetization capabilities across Paramount+ and Pluto TV. To drive strong alignment across these efforts, Robert Dumoulin, SVP of Applied Intelligence, will report to Barak.Technology will always be a creative multiplier and, with Barak’s leadership, I’m looking forward to seeing the impact he and the team will make as we drive our technological evolution forward. Please join me in welcoming Barak to Paramount!Cheers!Dane

results.title: Paramount Taps Former Google Exec as Consumer AI Head
highlights:
Paramount Taps Former Google Exec as Consumer AI Head

Paramount has hired former Google, Cisco and General Motors executive Barak Turovsky to serve as its executive vice president of consumer AI.

In his new role, Turovsky will be responsible for growing the media giant’s AI and machine learning teams and exploring ways to leverage AI to improve personalization, content discovery, platform intelligence, consumer engagement and monetization capabilities across Paramount+ and Pluto TV.

“As we see every day, the pace of technological change our industry is experiencing is rapid and we’ve already made tremendous progress in recent months to evolve our organization and products to remain on the forefront of what’s possible,” Paramount chief product officer Dane Glasgow said in a memo to staff on Monday. “Technology will always be a creative multiplier and, with Barak’s leadership, I’m looking forward to seeing the impact he and the team will make as we drive our technological evolution forward.”

Paramount senior vice president of applied intelligence Robert Dumoulin will report directly to Turovsky.

Read Next Paramount Chief Technology Officer Phil Wiser to Exit After 7 Years

Turovsky most recently served as chief AI officer at GM, where he led the automaker’s enterprise artificial intelligence strategy and deployment across its product, engineer, manufacturing and corporate functions.

Before that, he was vice president of AI at Cisco, leading product, research and engineering initiatives related to the technology. He was also chief product and technology officer at the computer vision AI solutions firm Trax.

Additionally, Turovsky spent more than a decade at Google, leading the Languages AI production organization, which was responsible for commercializing the technology across Google Search, Assistant, Ads, YouTube, Cloud AI, Translate, Android and other products.

Under his leadership, the tech giant scaled AI-powered experiences serving billions of users worldwide across multilingual understanding, semantic advertising, recommendation and ranking systems and enterprise AI services.

Read Next TV Writers Worry AI Will Replace Them. Now They’re Putting Those Anxieties on Screen

Turovsky joins Paramount as CEO David Ellison is looking to use AI and machine learning to transform the company into a technological leader in entertainment.

This also comes as Paramount+, Pluto TV and BET+ will launched on a unified backend infrastructure this summer. As part of the move, BET+ will be absorbed into Paramount+.

In an interview with TheWrap, Paramount chief revenue officer Jay Askinasi also teased a“complete evolution” of Pluto TV, with the FAST service’s refresh also set to take place this summer. That will include enhanced content discovery through automated carousels, Content Spotlight units, curated collections and Top 10 experiences.

Paramount+ is further expanding into new content formats and interactive features, such as the launch of clips on its mobile app, which have seen “high engagement” in initial beta testing. Executives also plan to add gaming, live stats and polling and are exploring adding AI-driven artwork.

“These investments all support our longterm DTC growth and are foundational to where we’re taking the business,” Ellison told analysts during the company’s first quarter earnings call last month. “These are all great learnings that will prepare us for the [Warner Bros. Discovery] transaction at the end of the third quarter.”

Read Next Paramount Focuses on Streaming Tech Transformation as WBD Deal Looms

results.title: Paramount Appoints Former Google Executive Barak Turovsky as Head of Consumer AI to Drive Digital Transformation | AlphaPilot
highlights:
Paramount Appoints Former Google Executive Barak Turovsky as Head of Consumer AI to Drive Digital Transformation | AlphaPilot
[...]
# Paramount Appoints Former Google Executive Barak Turovsky as Head of Consumer AI to Drive Digital Transformation
[...]
Paramount Global has hired tech veteran Barak Turovsky as Executive Vice President of Consumer AI to spearhead the integration of artificial intelligence across its streaming platforms and consumer applications under David Ellison's leadership.
[...]
Following the merger with Skydance, Paramount is aggressively pivoting toward a tech-first strategy by recruiting Barak Turovsky, who brings extensive experience from Google, Cisco, and General Motors. Turovsky will be responsible for embedding AI into the company's consumer-facing products, particularly Paramount+, to enhance user engagement and content discovery. This move comes amid a broader leadership shuffle, including the departure of the long-time Chief Technology Officer, signaling a significant shift in the company's digital roadmap. The market views this as a critical step for Paramount to remain competitive against tech-heavy rivals like Netflix and Disney.
[...]
Barak Turovsky is a former executive at Google, Cisco, and General Motors with deep expertise in AI and product leadership. He was hired to lead Paramount's consumer AI initiatives, focusing on improving the user experience across its digital ecosystem.
[...]
The appointment suggests a focus on using generative AI and machine learning to improve content discovery, search functionality, and personalized marketing on Paramount+, aiming to close the tech gap with competitors like NFLX.
[...]
This hire is a clear signal of David Ellison's intent to transform Paramount into a 'media-tech' hybrid, prioritizing digital innovation over traditional linear broadcasting models.
[...]
The appointment of Barak Turovsky is a strategic pivot that addresses Paramount's historical lag in technological infrastructure compared to Silicon Valley-backed streaming rivals. By pulling talent from GOOGL, Paramount is signaling that it no longer views itself as a traditional studio but as a data-driven platform. This move is particularly critical given the recent exit of the company's long-standing CTO; Turovsky represents the 'new guard' under the Skydance era, focused on consumer-facing AI rather than just backend maintenance. While the latest revenue figures show a modest base, the lack of insider selling over the past six months suggests a period of internal stabilization as the new leadership team takes shape. However, investors should monitor the tension between this high-tech push and the simultaneous loss of executives to companies like GPS and beehiiv. The success of this strategy will be measured by whether AI can tangibly improve the unit economics of Paramount+ through better ad-targeting and reduced subscriber acquisition costs, especially as the company navigates the high-cost environment of AI development.

# Manager, Internal Audit, Risk and Analytics, Investigations [Remote]
[...]
Posted Jun 9, 2026
[...]
The Manager, Risk & Data Analytics supports risk‑based audits, data‑driven insights, investigations, analytics, risk assessments, and special projects across Paramount's corporate, media, streaming, and production environments. The role focuses on evaluating processes, governance, and internal controls using data and judgment to identify risks, strengthen decision‑making, and deliver meaningful insights. The work includes operational reviews, T&E audits, production audits, and investigations. It also involves management-requested reviews and advisory projects. These tasks often require collaboration with SOX, Compliance, Legal, Finance, and other teams.
[...]
• Use advanced analytics for audit planning, testing, investigations, and risk monitoring
[...]
• Analyze large datasets (production costs, T&E, payroll, vendor payments, revenue) to identify trends and risks
[...]
• Translate risks into targeted analytics and communicate insights through visuals and dashboards
[...]
• Extensive experience analyzing large,
[...]
, or unstructured datasets
[...]
• Proficiency with analytics/visualization tools (SQL, Python, Power BI, Tableau, Alteryx, ACL)
[...]
• 7+ years in internal audit, external audit, risk management, forensic accounting, data analytics, or related fields
[...]
• Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or related field
[...]
• Comfort using AI‑enabled tools and advanced analytics
[...]
Paramount Skydance Corporation (NASDAQ: PSKY) is a leading global media and entertainment company that creates premium content and experiences for audiences worldwide. Driven by iconic studios, networks and streaming services, Paramount's portfolio of consumer brands includes CBS, Showtime Networks, Paramount Pictures, Nickelodeon, MTV, Comedy Central, BET, Paramount+, and Pluto TV, among others. Paramount delivers the largest share of the US television audience and boasts one of the industry's most important and extensive libraries of TV and film titles. In addition to offering innovative streaming services and digital video products, the company provides powerful capabilities in production, distribution and advertising solutions.

results.title: Sr Director, Finance Product Lead [Remote] | Quickswoop
highlights:
# Sr Director, Finance Product Lead [Remote]
[...]
As the Senior Director, Finance Product Lead, you will be the primary architect of the next generation of financial intelligence. Reporting to the SVP of ERP, you will occupy a unique ''Translator-in-Chief'' role-bridging the gap between the Office of the CFO and technical engineering teams.
[...]
Your mission is to transform our ERP from a static system of record into a dynamic engine of insight. You will lead the development of a comprehensive Finance Data Mart and serve as the Product Owner for a high-profile AI Analyst Bot. This role requires a rare blend of deep financial literacy, product management rigor, and the ability to steer elite engineering teams (both internal and external partners like Google) to deliver bleeding-edge variance analysis tools.
[...]
In this role, you aren't just managing software; you are redefining the role of the modern Finance professional. By automating the ''what'' and the ''why'' of financial variances through AI, you allow the CFO and FP&A teams to focus entirely on the ''what next''.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
1. Finance Data Mart & KPI Strategy

2. AI Innovation & Partner Integration

3. Engineering & Stakeholder Leadership
[...]
